text: A 15 cm skin incision was made at the level of the submarmarine groove following the scar of the previous operation.
The skin was retracted but not excised because viable and free of tumor.
The tumor was released all around from the subcutaneous tissue onto the muscular plane of the chest wall.
The pleural cavity was entered away from the tumor, one intercostal space above and one below the mass.
The third, fourth and fifth ribs were freed and surrounded with a tape.
Using a rib cutter, the mass was removed and blocked with a chest wall.
No adhesions were present with the underlying lung.
A resection margin of at least 2 cm were carefully maintained.
To reconstruct the chest wall, we used the titanium stratus system.
A polypropylene mesh was used to cover the chest wall defect.
Then, the titanium clips were applied to each rip stones and crimped using dedicated tools.
Clips with different angle were used for an optimal reconstruction of the third, fourth and fifth rips.
After checking the strength of the system, the titanium bars
previously tailored on the patient chest wall profile, were inserted below each pair of clips and crimped with dedicated tools.
A radon drain was placed in the subcutaneous space and the surgical incision was closed in several layers.
